# Memory Core Concept Discovery & Glossary Enhancement

## Objective
Use Memory Core session summaries, tickets, and the Knowledge Base to discover concepts not yet in the ontology — the "unknown unknowns."

## Context
Phase 3, Step 2 of the Concept Ontology epic (#10030). This is the ongoing maintenance and discovery layer. Depends on the integrated concept graph from #10035.

## Deliverables

### 1. Session-Based Concept Mining
Query the Memory Core for:
- Session summaries containing recurring architectural terms
- High strategic_weight topics from `get_context_frontier`
- Concepts that appear across 3+ sessions (Hebbian reinforcement signal)

Cross-reference against existing concept nodes. New terms that pass the Teaching Test → candidate concepts.

### 2. Ticket-Based Concept Mining
Scan GitHub issues (especially epics and architecture-labeled tickets) for:
- Architectural vocabulary not yet in the ontology
- Feature areas implicitly defining new concepts

### 3. KB-Assisted Classification
For UNCATEGORIZED classes (from #10032), use `ask_knowledge_base`:
```
"What architectural concept does Neo.core.EffectManager implement?"
→ "Pull-based reactivity / automatic dependency tracking"
```
If the answer identifies a concept not in the ontology → discovered unknown unknown.

### 4. Confidence Gating
- LLM-discovered concepts start at `tier: 3, confidence: 0.5`
- Only human curation or repeated session reinforcement promotes to higher tiers
- Prevents LLM noise from flooding the ontology

### 5. Glossary Enhancement (Downstream)
Once the concept graph is established, generate a diff showing:
- Concepts that should be in the Glossary but aren't
- Glossary terms that don't map to any concept (candidates for removal or reclassification)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Script or service can query Memory Core for concept candidates
- [ ] Discovered concepts correctly flagged with low confidence
- [ ] Human review gate exists before promotion
- [ ] Glossary gap report generated

## Note
This sub-issue requires the KB MCP server timeout issue to be resolved first — `ask_knowledge_base` currently times out due to ChromaDB cold-start latency exceeding the 60s MCP SDK default.

Origin Session ID: 496c0120-29aa-4b02-a804-d5a32ff6b5eb

- 2026-04-19T08:33:49Z @tobiu cross-referenced by PR #10078
- 2026-04-19T09:16:31Z @tobiu cross-referenced by #10080
- 2026-04-19T10:20:30Z @tobiu cross-referenced by PR #10084
- 2026-04-19T10:31:35Z @tobiu cross-referenced by #10086
- 2026-04-19T10:31:51Z @tobiu cross-referenced by #10087
- 2026-04-19T10:33:36Z @tobiu cross-referenced by #10030
- 2026-04-19T14:17:12Z @tobiu cross-referenced by PR #10100
- 2026-04-19T14:19:33Z @tobiu cross-referenced by #10085
- 2026-04-19T14:58:36Z @tobiu referenced in commit `b91d523` - "feat(ai): ORPHAN_CONCEPT signal + ⚠️ Orphaned Concepts handoff section (#10087)

Promotes orphan concept detection from the ephemeral per-orphan `logger.warn`
in `ConceptIngestor` to the durable `capabilityGap` tag channel + dedicated
`sandman_handoff.md` section — same pattern as `[GUIDE_GAP]` / `[EXAMPLE_GAP]`
established in #10084. Surfaces ontology data-quality issues where humans and
future agents can act on them rather than scroll past in daemon logs.

## Architecture

- `GapInferenceEngine.inferConceptGraphGaps` now emits a third weight-gated
  signal alongside `[GUIDE_GAP]` / `[EXAMPLE_GAP]`: `[ORPHAN_CONCEPT]` when a
  concept has no outbound `IMPLEMENTED_BY` edge. Orthogonal to the other two
  — a concept can be both orphaned and lack a guide. Gates via the shared
  `GUIDE_GAP_WEIGHT_THRESHOLD` so today's guide-title-derived ingestion (thin,
  mostly low-weight concepts) stays silent; the signal auto-surfaces as richer
  ingestion lands (#10036 / #10037 / #10050) without config changes.
- Action-biased entry text: "Concept X has no IMPLEMENTED_BY edge — either
  anchor it to a source file or retire the concept from nodes.jsonl if
  aspirational/stale."
- `ConceptIngestor.syncConceptsToGraph` deletes the per-orphan `logger.warn`
  (one line per orphan × N cycles = log spam growing linearly with ontology
  size). Retains `stats.orphansDetected` for the cycle-summary info line —
  which is the appropriate place for phase-level observability.
- `GoldenPathSynthesizer` parses the new `[ORPHAN_CONCEPT]` tag and renders
  a `### ⚠️ Orphaned Concepts` section with the same 5-item limit + TTL
  pruning as the other tag channels.

## Tests (39/39 stable × 5 runs)

- `DreamService.spec.mjs`: new test for three orphan-detection cases —
  fully-wired concept (no gaps), EXPLAINED_BY + EXEMPLIFIED_BY but no
  IMPLEMENTED_BY (ORPHAN_CONCEPT only, no GUIDE_GAP / EXAMPLE_GAP), and
  low-weight orphan (weight gate blocks emission).
- `DreamService.spec.mjs`: `synthesizeGoldenPath` test extended — planted a
  CONCEPT node with pre-written `[ORPHAN_CONCEPT]` capabilityGap; asserts
  the final handoff content includes the `⚠️ Orphaned Concepts` section.
- `DreamService.spec.mjs`: GUIDE_GAP covered-concept fixture now includes an
  IMPLEMENTED_BY edge (otherwise the fully-covered control concept would
  correctly emit the new ORPHAN_CONCEPT signal, inverting the test's premise).
- `ConceptIngestor.spec.mjs`: orphan test asserts `stats.orphansDetected`
  remains accurate AND no per-orphan logger.warn fires (the AC3 deletion).

## AC status

- [x] ConceptIngestor attaches orphan signal (now via GapInferenceEngine → capabilityGap) — the architectural home for concept-gap emission
- [x] GoldenPathSynthesizer categorizes `[ORPHAN_CONCEPT]` + renders dedicated section
- [x] logger.warn removed entirely (graph persistence is the durable signal)
- [x] Existing tests still pass — 39/39 stable
- [x] New test: ingesting an orphan concept causes `⚠️ Orphaned Concepts` section to appear in handoff

Origin Session ID: 1db25bbe-f39d-4dcd-bb0e-bc125ce91326

* test(ai): co-emission spec + symmetric DreamService afterEach (#10087)

Addresses the optional follow-ups from #10101's self-review:

- New test for GUIDE_GAP + ORPHAN_CONCEPT co-emission on a concept lacking
  BOTH EXPLAINED_BY and IMPLEMENTED_BY. Locks in the branch orthogonality —
  the GUIDE_GAP / EXAMPLE_GAP pair is mutually exclusive, but ORPHAN_CONCEPT
  is independent. The three-case test (fully-wired / orphan-only / low-weight)
  didn't hit the compound case directly; this does.

- Symmetric `afterEach` in DreamService.spec.mjs mirroring the existing
  `beforeEach` graph-state cleanup. Applies the `feedback_symmetric_spec_cleanup.md`
  discipline established in #10100: `fullyParallel: true` interleaves specs
  in the same worker, and `GraphService.db` is a shared singleton. Without
  `afterEach`, the last test's fixtures (CONCEPT nodes planted by the
  ORPHAN_CONCEPT detection tests) leak to whatever test Playwright queues
  next from another spec. The existing `afterAll` nullifies `GraphService.db`
  so cross-spec leak is bounded at worker-shutdown, but per-test symmetric
  cleanup closes the door at the test-boundary level — belt-and-suspenders,
  aligned with the discipline I just codified.

40/40 tests pass across 5 consecutive runs.

Origin Session ID: 1db25bbe-f39d-4dcd-bb0e-bc125ce91326"
- 2026-04-19T15:20:04Z @tobiu cross-referenced by PR #10102
- 2026-04-19T15:41:23Z @tobiu assigned to @tobiu
- 2026-04-19T15:58:56Z @tobiu referenced in commit `695cc34` - "feat(ai): ConceptDiscoveryService + validated flag for mined candidates (#10036)

Addresses deliverables 1, 2, and 4 of #10036 in one cohesive slice per the
intake discussion:

- **Deliverable 1 (session-based mining)**: scans Memory Core session summaries
  via StorageRouter.getSummaryCollection() for PascalCase multi-word phrases
  recurring across ≥ MIN_FREQUENCY (3) distinct summaries.
- **Deliverable 2 (issue-based mining)**: scans local GitHub issue markdown
  (resources/content/issues/*.md) with the same regex + Hebbian floor.
- **Deliverable 4 (confidence gating)**: new `validated: boolean` field on
  concept nodes + explicit gate in GapInferenceEngine.inferConceptGraphGaps.
  Legacy rows without the field (`undefined`) treated as validated (no data
  migration). Mined candidates carry `validated: false`, tier 3, default
  weight → silenced in sandman_handoff.md via two independent mechanisms
  (weight gate + validated gate). Curator promotion = edit nodes.jsonl:
  flip validated + bump tier/weight. Git diff is the review interface.

Deliverables 3 (KB-assisted classification — KB MCP timeout blocker per ticket
body) and 5 (glossary gap report — pure reporting tool) deferred until value
materializes.

## Architecture

Single `nodes.jsonl` stays the source of truth — no staging file (per #10036
intake: dual-file would duplicate the curator's review surface). Weight gate
+ validated flag silence candidates until review.

ConceptDiscoveryService is the reverse-flow proposal channel to ConceptIngestor's
additive one-way sync — split by data-flow direction (JSONL ← discovery;
JSONL → graph). Combining would conflate trust levels.

Extraction: deterministic regex for PascalCase multi-word phrases (2-4 words),
strips leading articles/determiners ("The Concept Ontology" → "Concept
Ontology"), applies stop-phrase blocklist, dedupes against
ConceptService.aliasIndex + nodes. No LLM — offline-safe, cheap, sufficient
for the Hebbian reinforcement signal the ticket specifies.

Runs standalone via `DreamService.runConceptDiscovery()`; not yet wired into
the REM-cycle loop (deliberate — lets curator invoke deliberately rather than
flooding the JSONL on every cycle).

## Tests (48/48 stable × 5 runs)

- `ConceptDiscoveryService.spec.mjs`: 6 new tests covering extraction (PascalCase
  + YAML frontmatter + leading-article strip), filter (MIN_FREQUENCY + stop-
  phrases + ConceptService dedupe), session mining (StorageRouter stub), issue
  mining (temp markdown files), end-to-end runDiscoveryCycle + JSONL append,
  and idempotency across consecutive runs.
- `DreamService.spec.mjs`: new test proving `validated: false` silences all
  three concept-graph signals regardless of weight (weight 1.3 > 0.8 threshold
  would normally emit GUIDE_GAP + ORPHAN_CONCEPT; gate holds).
- Symmetric afterEach in the new spec per `feedback_symmetric_spec_cleanup.md`.

## AC Checklist (deliverables 1, 2, 4)

- [x] Script or service can query Memory Core for concept candidates
      (ConceptDiscoveryService.mineFromMemoryCore)
- [x] Discovered concepts correctly flagged with low confidence
      (validated: false, tier: 3, default weight under 0.8 threshold)
- [x] Human review gate exists before promotion
      (weight gate + validated gate; curator flips flag via JSONL edit)
- [x] Existing tests still pass (40 → 48; all stable × 5 runs)

Origin Session ID: 1db25bbe-f39d-4dcd-bb0e-bc125ce91326"
- 2026-04-19T16:05:15Z @tobiu cross-referenced by PR #10105
- 2026-04-19T16:20:52Z @tobiu referenced in commit `8613143` - "feat(ai): ConceptDiscoveryService (LLM-based) + validated flag (#10036)

Lands deliverables 1, 2, and 4 of #10036 in one cohesive slice per the intake
discussion.

## Approach

LLM-driven concept extraction via `OpenAiCompatible.generate` (gemma4 or
configured provider — same substrate used by `SemanticGraphExtractor` and
`GoldenPathSynthesizer`). Two high-signal corpora:

- **Epics** — issue markdown files labeled `epic` in `resources/content/issues/`.
  Epic bodies are curated distilled architecture.
- **Pull requests** — `resources/content/pulls/*.md`, including their
  `## Comments` section. PR review comments contain `[ARCH_ALIGNMENT]` /
  `[RETROSPECTIVE]` / `[KB_GAP]` / "Gold Standards" / "Traps Avoided" prose —
  the exact architectural discourse where new vocabulary emerges. Sorted
  descending by PR number (freshest discourse first), capped at
  `DEFAULT_PR_SCAN_LIMIT = 20` per cycle to bound LLM cost.

The system prompt encodes the Teaching Test + explicit anti-patterns (class
names, file paths, framework names without architectural weight, routine
ticket vocabulary). Output is strict JSON parsed via `Json.extract` for
fence-tolerance. Per-source malformed output is logged and skipped — never
fatal to the cycle.

## Schema (Deliverable 4)

- `validated: boolean` field on concept nodes. Default `undefined` (legacy)
  treated as validated (no data migration). Mined candidates carry
  `validated: false`, tier 3, default weight → silenced in sandman_handoff.md
  via two independent mechanisms (weight gate + validated gate).
- `ConceptIngestor.computePayloadHash` includes validated, so flipping
  curator-review state triggers re-upsert.
- `ConceptIngestor` upsert-properties include validated, propagating to graph.
- `GapInferenceEngine.inferConceptGraphGaps` skips `validated === false`
  concepts regardless of weight — belt-and-suspenders alongside the weight
  gate. All three signals (GUIDE_GAP / EXAMPLE_GAP / ORPHAN_CONCEPT) respect
  the gate.
- Curator promotion = edit nodes.jsonl: flip `validated: true` + bump tier
  and/or weight. Git diff is the review surface.

## Tests (48/48 stable × 5 runs)

- `ConceptDiscoveryService.spec.mjs` (6 tests): epic-label filtering,
  PR cap + recency ordering, ConceptService dedupe (by id + alias),
  malformed-LLM tolerance, MIN_SOURCE_LENGTH short-circuit, end-to-end
  runDiscoveryCycle + JSONL append. Mocks `OpenAiCompatible.prototype.generate`
  with queued responses per call. Symmetric afterEach per
  `feedback_symmetric_spec_cleanup.md`.
- `DreamService.spec.mjs` new test: `validated: false` silences all three
  concept-graph signals even with weight 1.3 > 0.8 threshold.

## Deferred (out of this PR)

- **Deliverable 3** (KB-assisted classification) — blocked on KB MCP timeout
  per ticket body.
- **Deliverable 5** (glossary gap report) — pure reporting tool; file when
  ontology matures.
- **REM-cycle integration** — manual invocation via
  `DreamService.runConceptDiscovery()` for now. Per-cycle LLM cost would
  flood nodes.jsonl; deliberate to let curator/operator set cadence.
- **Session-summary mining** — epics + PRs are higher signal; add
  session-based mining as a follow-up if epic/PR coverage proves insufficient.

Origin Session ID: 1db25bbe-f39d-4dcd-bb0e-bc125ce91326"
- 2026-04-19T16:26:28Z @tobiu referenced in commit `4df4b09` - "feat(ai): config-lift ConceptDiscoveryService tuning knobs (#10036)

Addresses tobi's polish catch on PR #10105: `DEFAULT_PR_SCAN_LIMIT = 20` and
`MIN_SOURCE_LENGTH = 200` were file-local magic numbers. Operator-tuning knobs
belong in config, not as consts buried in the service. Same discipline as
#10086 (`guideGapWeightThreshold` config-lift).

## Scope

- `config.template.mjs` + local `config.mjs`: new `conceptDiscovery` nested
  object grouping `prScanLimit` (default 20) + `minSourceLength` (default 200).
  Env overrides via `NEO_CONCEPT_DISCOVERY_PR_SCAN_LIMIT` +
  `NEO_CONCEPT_DISCOVERY_MIN_SOURCE_LENGTH`. Nested grouping matches existing
  `openAiCompatible` / `ollama` convention for service-scoped configs.
- `ConceptDiscoveryService.mjs`: dropped the two constants. Reads from
  `aiConfig.data.conceptDiscovery?.prScanLimit ?? 20` +
  `aiConfig.data.conceptDiscovery?.minSourceLength ?? 200` at call time (live
  reads, not module-load capture — enables runtime tuning + test overrides
  without reimport). Instance-level `prScanLimit` field retained for test
  overrides (falls through to aiConfig when null).
- Parity: local `config.mjs` mirrors the template addition (gitignored per
  `.gitignore`; not committed — only template goes into git).

## Out of scope (tracked for #10103)

Per `feedback_config_service_boundary.md` + #10103: these keys belong on the
SDK-layer config (ConceptDiscoveryService is a daemon service, not a memory-
core concern). Placed in memory-core config as the pragmatic destination today
— same debt as `guideGapWeightThreshold`. Migration is #10103's scope.

## Tests (49/49 stable × 3 runs)

New test: `aiConfig.data.conceptDiscovery.minSourceLength` override changes
extraction behavior. Proves the config is read at call time (raise threshold
above body length → LLM skipped; restore default → LLM invoked).

Origin Session ID: 1db25bbe-f39d-4dcd-bb0e-bc125ce91326"

Origin Session ID: 1db25bbe-f39d-4dcd-bb0e-bc125ce91326"
- 2026-04-19T16:30:19Z @tobiu closed this issue
- 2026-04-19T17:47:59Z @tobiu cross-referenced by #10106
